Personal Homepage
Scholar | Educator | Research Leader

Dr Zerihun Doda

Associate Professor of Sociology and Anthropology, Research Director, and Senior Faculty Member at Ethiopian Public Service University

I am an academic and researcher with more than 28 years of experience in teaching, research, training, and consultancy. My work focuses on social protection, public service delivery, sustainable development, conflict and peace, climate change, and biocultural diversity conservation.

Based in: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia
Education: PhD in Anthropology & Biodiversity Management, University of Kent, UK
Research Profiles: Web of Science: V-7455-2018 | ORCID: 0000-0001-9545-8994 | Google Scholar: cited by 329 overall, 230 since 2021; h-index 10 overall, 9 since 2021; i10-index 11 overall, 9 since 2021

Career Timeline

A compact view of the major academic, institutional, and leadership phases of my career.

1996-2004
Dilla University Progressed from Graduate Assistant to Lecturer in Sociology and Anthropology, later coordinating research and publication work.
2004-2011
Hawassa University Taught widely across sociology, anthropology, policy, and development, while strengthening teaching, research, and public engagement.
2011-2015
University of Kent, United Kingdom Completed a PhD in Anthropology and Biodiversity Management with specialization in biocultural diversity conservation.
2016-2018
Return to Hawassa University Served as Assistant Professor, chaired journal-establishment work, and supported editorial leadership and interdisciplinary teaching.
2018-Present
Ethiopian Public Service University Associate Professor, Senior Faculty Member, Research Director, Editor-in-Chief, doctoral teacher, and national reform contributor.

Publications & Research

A snapshot of current research, national reform leadership, doctoral teaching, and public scholarship across sociology, anthropology, governance, environment, and public service.

  • Current Research Enhancing pro-environmental behaviors in the manufacturing sector; child migrant laborers from Wolayta Zone in Addis Ababa; and advanced work on public sector reform, ethics, and service delivery.
  • Recent National Leadership Secretary and editor-in-chief for the Ethiopian Public Service University reorientation study national taskforce; contributor to the Ethiopian Management Institute reorientation taskforce; and lead on the University's research, technology transfer, and community engagement reform work.
  • Doctoral Teaching & Service Advanced Research Methodology and advanced qualitative data analysis software training, supervision, and examination support for PhD students across multiple Ethiopian universities and for UNISA examination work.
  • Public Scholarship Media interviews on national reconciliation, childhood graduation culture, and solidarity-based social support during Ethiopian festive seasons on Addis Media Network and Finfine FM.
  • Selected Publication Belda, F., Keno, G. I., Lemessa, A., & Doda, Z. (2025). The course of industrial development influencing smallholder farmers' peace in the face of structural and cultural violence. Journal of Rural and Community Development, 20(2), 125-147.

Courses Taught

Courses taught across sociology, anthropology, research methods, and public policy related fields.

  • Contemporary Discourses in Gender, Climate Change and Conflict
  • Sociology of Conflict and Peace
  • Advanced Qualitative Research Methods (CAQDAS)
  • Ecological Anthropology, Social Policy Analysis, Urban Sociology, and Disaster Risk Management

Research, Reform & National Taskforces

Recent and representative research, institutional reform, and national assignment roles.

  • Enhancing Pro-Environmental Behaviors in the Manufacturing Sector (Co-Investigator, Ethiopian Public Service University, 2024-Present)
  • Child Migrant Laborers from Wolayta Zone in Addis Ababa (Principal Investigator, Ethiopian Public Service University, 2024-Present)
  • Ethiopian Public Service University Reorientation Study National Taskforce Member and Secretary; editor-in-chief for report and policy brief production (since November 2024).
  • National taskforce contributor for the reorientation of the Ethiopian Management Institute, contributing to a long-range strategic roadmap.
  • Lead for the University's grand reform work on Research, Technology Transfer and Community Engagement: AS-IS analysis, TO-BE design, organizational structure, job descriptions, competency assessment, and redeployment framework (since November 2025).

Program Design, Journals & Media

Recent contributions in doctoral program design, journal development, editorial leadership, and public-facing academic service.

  • Member of the PhD Program Design Consulting Team in Transformational Leadership and Holistic Development for the Global Institute of Transformational Leadership since October 2024.
  • Designed courses including Advanced Research Methods, Anthropology of Development, and PhD Dissertation Seminars.
  • Member of the journal establishment team for the International Journal of Transformational Leadership and Holistic Development; led editorial policy development and manuscript review toward publishable issues.
  • Voluntary and professional media appearances in 2024-2025 on Addis Media Network and Finfine FM on national reconciliation, kindergarten graduation culture, and solidarity-based social support during Ethiopian festive seasons.

Doctoral Teaching & Examination

Advanced doctoral teaching, software training, supervision, and examination service across universities and programs.

  • Taught Advanced Research Methodology and advanced qualitative data analysis using NVivo and MAXQDA for PhD students at Ethiopian Public Service University, Arba Minch University, Hawassa University, Addis Ababa University (Center for Human Rights), and Haramaya University.
  • Supervised PhD students at Ethiopian Public Service University, Haramaya University, and Hawassa University.
  • Served as external examiner for over dozens of PhD dissertations and several dozens of MA theses across multiple universities, including Addis Ababa University.
  • Most recent international doctoral examination service: University of South Africa (UNISA).

Journal Articles

Selected journal articles and public article records associated with my work. Entries are linked where a public journal page, DOI, or repository record is available.

Showing all visible records
  1. Belda, F., Keno, G. I., Lemessa, A., & Doda, Z. (2025). The course of industrial development influencing smallholder farmers' peace in the face of structural and cultural violence: The case of Lume District, Oromia, Ethiopia. Journal of Rural and Community Development, 20(2), 125-147.
  2. Fufa, Y., Desalegn, T. A., Abera, N., Getu, A., Tufa, Y. A., Demlie, Y. W., ... Doffana, Z. D., et al. (2025). Anthrax outbreak surveillance and response in Arba Minch, Ethiopia: After-action review. Journal of Public Health in Africa, 16(1), 1293.
  3. Belda, F., Imana, G., Lemessa, A., & Doda, Z. (2024). Land acquisition for industrial development and its repercussions on the peace of the local farming communities in Lume District, Oromia, Ethiopia. Cogent Social Sciences, 10(1), Article 2306031.
  4. Doda, Z., & Worku, M. (2023). Public sector greenness and cleanliness in Ethiopia: A case study of government organizations in Adama and Bahir Dar Cities, Ethiopia. Journal of African Development Studies, 10(2), 75-90.
  5. Shete, M., Doffana, Z. D., & Abate, S. M. (2023). The state and drivers of corruption in the Ethiopian revenue sector. Journal of Business and Administrative Studies, 15(1), 15-39.
  6. Tufa, Y. A., Fufa, Y., Damene, W., Takele, A., Ayele, B., Doffana, Z., et al. (2023). Qualitative review of dengue fever outbreak in Dire Dawa City, Ethiopia, 2021: A thematic analysis approach. Cogent Public Health, 10(1).
  7. Endris, J., Gutema, I., Doda, Z., & Lemessa, A. (2022). Resilience of people displaced from Ethio-Somali Region and resettled in Oromia Special Zone surrounding Finfinne (Addis Ababa), Ethiopia. African Journal of Social Work, 12(6), 325-335.
  8. Endris Jafer, Gutema Imana, Zerihun Doda, & Abebe Lemessa. (2022). Post conflict-induced displacement: Human security challenges of internally displaced persons in Sululta, Oromia Special Zone Surrounding Finfinne, Ethiopia. Cogent Social Sciences, 8(1).
  9. Doda, Z. (2021). Socio-cultural considerations in environmental policy formulation and implementation in Ethiopia. Journal of African Development Studies, 8(2), 38-49.
  10. Tufa, Y. A., Fufa, Y., Abebe, M., Habebe, S., Sufa, D., ... & Doda, Z. (2020). Qualitative research on yellow fever outbreak responses in Wolaita Zone of SNNPR Region, Ethiopia. American Journal of Health Research, 8(3), 32-41.
  11. Alemayehu, D., & Doda, Z. (2020). Indigenous environmental knowledge of Borana pastoralists. Grassroots Journal of Natural Resources, 3(4), 110-131.
  12. Doffana, Z. D. (2019). Sacred sites and ancestor veneration in Sidama, southwest Ethiopia: A socio-ecological perspective. Cogent Social Sciences, 5(1).
  13. Doda, Z. (2019). The conservation of African yellowwood tree (Afrocarpus falcatus) in Sidama sacred sites, Ethiopia. Cogent Social Sciences, 5(1), 1-17.
  14. Doda, Z. (2018). Indigenous religions, identity and biodiversity in Southwest Ethiopia: Lessons from Council of Nationalities-SNNPRS commissioned studies. ITYOPIS: Northeast African Journal of Social Sciences and Humanities, Extra Issue III.
  15. Doda, Z., Solomon, T., Loha, E., Gari, T., & Lindtjorn, B. (2018). A qualitative study of use of long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs) for intended and unintended purposes in Adami Tullu, South Central Ethiopia. Malaria Journal, 17(1), 69.
  16. Doffana, Z. D. (2017). Sacred natural sites, herbal medicine, medicinal plants and their conservation in Sidama, Ethiopia. Cogent Food & Agriculture, 3(1), 1365399.

Books & Chapters

Selected books, chapters, and related public book records connected to my work.

  1. Doda, Z. (2022/2023). Preface to The Sidaama, edited by Sophia Thubauville and Anbessa Teferra. Public Google Play book page.
  2. Doffana, Z. (2018). The role of sacred natural sites in conflict resolution: Lessons from the Wonsho sacred forests of Sidama, Ethiopia. Chapter in The SAGE Handbook of Nature.

Reports & Theses

Dissertation, thesis, and commissioned-study records connected to my work. Where a publisher-hosted page was not available, I used the most stable public record I could locate.

  1. Doffana, Z. D. (2014). ‘Dagucho [Podocarpus falcatus] Is Abbo!’ Wonsho Sacred Sites, Sidama, Ethiopia: Origins, Maintenance Motives, Consequences and Conservation Threats. PhD thesis, University of Kent.
  2. Doda, Z. (2010/2014 public record). The Ethnohistory & Culture of Tambaro Nationality of Ethiopia. Commissioned ethno-historical study and public document page.
  3. Doffana, Z. D. (2009/2010 public record). Ethno-History of Baskeš Nationality. Public document page for an ethnographic and historiographical study of the Baskeš/Basketo of southwest Ethiopia.
  4. Doda, Z. (2001). Culture & Mental Illness among the Zay of Lake Zway Islands, Ethiopia. MA thesis, Addis Ababa University. Publicly documented in the author's CV and related course references.
